 48→/**
 49→ * Linting trailing comma for declaration site.
 50→ *
 51→ * @see [Kotlin Style Guide](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html#trailing-commas)
 52→ */
 53→public class TrailingCommaOnDeclarationSiteRule :
 54→    Rule(
 55→        id = "trailing-comma-on-declaration-site",
 56→        visitorModifiers = setOf(
 57→            VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule(
 58→                ruleId = "standard:wrapping",
 59→                loadOnlyWhenOtherRuleIsLoaded = true,
 60→                runOnlyWhenOtherRuleIsEnabled = true,
 61→            ),
 62→            VisitorModifier.RunAsLateAsPossible,
 63→        ),
 64→    ),
 65→    UsesEditorConfigProperties {
 66→
 67→    override val editorConfigProperties: List<EditorConfigProperty<*>> = listOf(
 68→        TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_PROPERTY,
 69→    )
 70→
 71→    private var allowTrailingComma by Delegates.notNull<Boolean>()
 72→
 73→    private fun ASTNode.isTrailingCommaAllowed() =
 74→        elementType in TYPES_ON_DECLARATION_SITE && allowTrailingComma
 75→
 76→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfigProperties: EditorConfigProperties) {
 77→        allowTrailingComma = editorConfigProperties.getEditorConfigValue(TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_PROPERTY)
 78→    }
 79→
 80→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 81→        node: ASTNode,
 82→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 83→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 84→    ) {
 85→        // Keep processing of element types in sync with Intellij Kotlin formatting settings.
 86→        // https://github.com/JetBrains/intellij-kotlin/blob/master/formatter/src/org/jetbrains/kotlin/idea/formatter/trailingComma/util.kt
 87→        when (node.elementType) {
 88→            CLASS -> visitClass(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 89→            DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION -> visitDestructuringDeclaration(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 90→            FUNCTION_LITERAL -> visitFunctionLiteral(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 91→            TYPE_PARAMETER_LIST -> visitTypeList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 92→            VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST -> visitValueList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 93→            WHEN_ENTRY -> visitWhenEntry(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 94→            else -> Unit
 95→        }
 96→    }
 97→
 98→    private fun visitDestructuringDeclaration(
 99→        node: ASTNode,
100→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
101→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
102→    ) {
103→        val inspectNode = node
104→            .children()
105→            .last { it.elementType == ElementType.RPAR }
106→        node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
107→            inspectNode = inspectNode,
108→            isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
109→            autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
110→            emit = emit,
111→        )
112→    }
113→
114→    private fun visitFunctionLiteral(
115→        node: ASTNode,
116→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
117→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
118→    ) {
119→        val inspectNode = node
120→            .children()
121→            .lastOrNull { it.elementType == ARROW }
122→            ?: // lambda w/o an arrow -> no arguments -> no commas
123→            return
124→        node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
125→            inspectNode = inspectNode,
126→            isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
127→            autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
128→            emit = emit,
129→        )
130→    }
131→
132→    private fun visitValueList(
133→        node: ASTNode,
134→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
135→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
136→    ) {
137→        if (node.treeParent.elementType != FUNCTION_LITERAL) {
138→            node
139→                .children()
140→                .lastOrNull { it.elementType == ElementType.RPAR }
141→                ?.let { inspectNode ->
142→                    node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
143→                        inspectNode = inspectNode,
144→                        isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
145→                        autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
146→                        emit = emit,
147→                    )
148→                }
149→        }
150→    }
151→
152→    private fun visitTypeList(
153→        node: ASTNode,
154→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
155→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
156→    ) {
157→        val inspectNode = node
158→            .children()
159→            .first { it.elementType == ElementType.GT }
160→        node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
161→            inspectNode = inspectNode,
162→            isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
163→            autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
164→            emit = emit,
165→        )
166→    }
167→
168→    private fun visitWhenEntry(
169→        node: ASTNode,
170→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
171→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
172→    ) {
173→        val psi = node.psi
174→        require(psi is KtWhenEntry)
175→        if (psi.isElse || psi.parent.cast<KtWhenExpression>().leftParenthesis == null) {
176→            // no commas for "else" or when there are no opening parenthesis for the when-expression
177→            return
178→        }
179→
180→        val inspectNode = node
181→            .children()
182→            .first { it.elementType == ARROW }
183→        node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
184→            inspectNode = inspectNode,
185→            isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
186→            autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
187→            emit = emit,
188→        )
189→    }
190→    private fun visitClass(
191→        node: ASTNode,
192→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
193→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
194→    ) {
195→        val psi = node.psi
196→        require(psi is KtClass)
197→
198→        node
199→            .takeIf { psi.isEnum() }
200→            ?.findChildByType(ElementType.CLASS_BODY)
201→            ?.takeUnless {
202→                // Do nothing when last two entries are on same line as no trailing comma should be inserted
203→                it.lastTwoEnumEntriesAreOnSameLine()
204→            }?.let { classBody ->
205→                val nodeAfterTrailingCommaPosition = classBody.findNodeAfterTrailingCommaPosition()
206→                node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
207→                    inspectNode = nodeAfterTrailingCommaPosition,
208→                    isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
209→                    autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
210→                    emit = emit,
211→                )
212→            }
213→    }
214→
215→    /**
216→     * Determines the [ASTNode] before which the trailing comma is allowed.
217→     *
218→     * If the list of enumeration entries is terminated by a semicolon, that semicolon will be returned. Otherwise, the
219→     * last element of the class.
220→     */
221→    private fun ASTNode.findNodeAfterTrailingCommaPosition(): ASTNode {
222→        val lastEnumEntry = children().last { it.psi is KtEnumEntry }
223→
224→        val semicolonAfterLastEnumEntry = lastEnumEntry
225→            .children()
226→            .singleOrNull { it.elementType == ElementType.SEMICOLON }
227→
228→        return semicolonAfterLastEnumEntry ?: lastChildNode
229→    }
230→
231→    private fun ASTNode.lastTwoEnumEntriesAreOnSameLine(): Boolean {
232→        val lastTwoEnumEntries =
233→            children()
234→                .filter { it.psi is KtEnumEntry }
235→                .toList()
236→                .takeLast(2)
237→
238→        return lastTwoEnumEntries.count() == 2 && noNewLineInClosedRange(lastTwoEnumEntries[0], lastTwoEnumEntries[1])
239→    }
240→
241→    private fun ASTNode.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
242→        inspectNode: ASTNode,
243→        isTrailingCommaAllowed: Boolean,
244→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
245→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
246→    ) {
247→        val prevLeaf = inspectNode.prevLeaf()
248→        val trailingCommaNode = prevLeaf?.findPreviousTrailingCommaNodeOrNull()
249→        val trailingCommaState = when {
250→            isMultiline(psi) -> if (trailingCommaNode != null) TrailingCommaState.EXISTS else TrailingCommaState.MISSING
251→            else -> if (trailingCommaNode != null) TrailingCommaState.REDUNDANT else TrailingCommaState.NOT_EXISTS
252→        }
253→        when (trailingCommaState) {
254→            TrailingCommaState.EXISTS ->
255→                if (isTrailingCommaAllowed) {
256→                    inspectNode
257→                        .treeParent
258→                        .takeIf { it.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY }
259→                        ?.findChildByType(ARROW)
260→                        ?.prevLeaf()
261→                        ?.let { lastNodeBeforeArrow ->
262→                            if (lastNodeBeforeArrow.elementType != WHITE_SPACE || !lastNodeBeforeArrow.textContains('\n')) {
263→                                emit(
264→                                    trailingCommaNode!!.startOffset,
265→                                    "Expected a newline between the trailing comma and  \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
266→                                    true,
267→                                )
268→                                if (autoCorrect) {
269→                                    val parentIndent = "\n" + inspectNode.getWhenEntryIndent()
270→                                    lastNodeBeforeArrow.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(parentIndent)
271→                                }
272→                            }
273→                        }
274→                } else {
275→                    emit(
276→                        trailingCommaNode!!.startOffset,
277→                        "Unnecessary trailing comma before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
278→                        true,
279→                    )
280→                    if (autoCorrect) {
281→                        this.removeChild(trailingCommaNode)
282→                    }
283→                }
284→            TrailingCommaState.MISSING ->
285→                if (isTrailingCommaAllowed) {
286→                    val addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhenEntry = addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhen()
287→                    val prevNode = inspectNode.prevCodeLeaf()!!
288→                    if (addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhenEntry) {
289→                        emit(
290→                            prevNode.startOffset + prevNode.textLength,
291→                            "Missing trailing comma and newline before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
292→                            true,
293→                        )
294→                    } else {
295→                        emit(
296→                            prevNode.startOffset + prevNode.textLength,
297→                            "Missing trailing comma before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
298→                            true,
299→                        )
300→                    }
301→                    if (autoCorrect) {
302→                        if (addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhenEntry) {
303→                            val parentIndent = "\n" + prevNode.getWhenEntryIndent()
304→                            val leafBeforeArrow = (psi as KtWhenEntry).arrow?.prevLeaf()
305→                            if (leafBeforeArrow != null && leafBeforeArrow is PsiWhiteSpace) {
306→                                val newLine = KtPsiFactory(prevNode.psi).createWhiteSpace(parentIndent)
307→                                leafBeforeArrow.replace(newLine)
308→                            } else {
309→                                val newLine = KtPsiFactory(prevNode.psi).createWhiteSpace(parentIndent)
310→                                prevNode.psi.parent.addAfter(newLine, prevNode.psi)
311→                            }
312→                        }
313→
314→                        if (inspectNode.treeParent.elementType == ElementType.ENUM_ENTRY) {
315→                            with(KtPsiFactory(prevNode.psi)) {
316→                                val parentIndent =
317→                                    (prevNode.psi.parent.prevLeaf() as? PsiWhiteSpace)?.text
318→                                        ?: "\n${prevNode.lineIndent()}"
319→                                val newline = createWhiteSpace(parentIndent)
320→                                val enumEntry = inspectNode.treeParent.psi
321→                                enumEntry.apply {
322→                                    add(newline)
323→                                    removeChild(inspectNode)
324→                                    parent.addAfter(createSemicolon(), this)
325→                                }
326→                            }
327→                        }
328→
329→                        val comma = KtPsiFactory(prevNode.psi).createComma()
330→                        prevNode.psi.parent.addAfter(comma, prevNode.psi)
331→                    }
332→                }
333→            TrailingCommaState.REDUNDANT -> {
334→                emit(
335→                    trailingCommaNode!!.startOffset,
336→                    "Unnecessary trailing comma before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
337→                    true,
338→                )
339→                if (autoCorrect) {
340→                    this.removeChild(trailingCommaNode)
341→                }
342→            }
343→            TrailingCommaState.NOT_EXISTS -> Unit
344→        }
345→    }
346→
347→    private fun ASTNode.getWhenEntryIndent() =
348→        // The when entry can be a simple value but might also be a complex expression.
349→        parents()
350→            .last { it.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY }
351→            .lineIndent()
352→
353→    private fun isMultiline(element: PsiElement): Boolean = when {
354→        element.parent is KtFunctionLiteral -> isMultiline(element.parent)
355→        element is KtFunctionLiteral -> containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(element.valueParameterList!!, element.arrow!!)
356→        element is KtWhenEntry -> containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(element.firstChild, element.arrow!!)
357→        element is KtDestructuringDeclaration -> containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(element.lPar!!, element.rPar!!)
358→        element is KtValueArgumentList && element.children.size == 1 && element.anyDescendantOfType<KtCollectionLiteralExpression>() -> {
359→            // special handling for collection literal
360→            // @Annotation([
361→            //    "something",
362→            // ])
363→            val lastChild = element.collectDescendantsOfType<KtCollectionLiteralExpression>().last()
364→            containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(lastChild.rightBracket!!, element.rightParenthesis!!)
365→        }
366→        element is KtParameterList && element.parameters.isEmpty() -> false
367→        else -> element.textContains('\n')
368→    }
369→
370→    private fun ASTNode.addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhen() =
371→        if (psi is KtWhenEntry) {
372→            val leafBeforeArrow = (psi as KtWhenEntry).arrow?.prevLeaf()
373→            !(leafBeforeArrow is PsiWhiteSpace && leafBeforeArrow.textContains('\n'))
374→        } else {
375→            false
376→        }
377→
378→    private fun ASTNode.findPreviousTrailingCommaNodeOrNull(): ASTNode? {
379→        val codeLeaf = if (isCodeLeaf()) {
380→            this
381→        } else {
382→            prevCodeLeaf()
383→        }
384→        return codeLeaf?.takeIf { it.elementType == ElementType.COMMA }
385→    }
386→
387→    private fun containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(from: PsiElement, to: PsiElement): Boolean {
388→        var leaf: PsiElement? = from
389→        while (leaf != null && !leaf.isEquivalentTo(to)) {
390→            if (leaf.textContains('\n')) {
391→                return true
392→            }
393→            leaf = leaf.nextLeaf(skipEmptyElements = false)
394→        }
395→        return leaf?.textContains('\n') ?: false
396→    }
397→
398→    private enum class TrailingCommaState {
399→        /**
400→         * The trailing comma is needed and exists
401→         */
402→        EXISTS,
403→
404→        /**
405→         * The trailing comma is needed and doesn't exist
406→         */
407→        MISSING,
408→
409→        /**
410→         * The trailing comma isn't needed and doesn't exist
411→         */
412→        NOT_EXISTS,
413→
414→        /**
415→         * The trailing comma isn't needed, but exists
416→         */
417→        REDUNDANT,
418→    }
419→
420→    public companion object {
421→        private val BOOLEAN_VALUES_SET = setOf("true", "false")
422→
423→        public val TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_PROPERTY: EditorConfigProperty<Boolean> =
424→            EditorConfigProperty(
425→                type = PropertyType.LowerCasingPropertyType(
426→                    "ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma",
427→                    "Defines whether a trailing comma (or no trailing comma) should be enforced on the defining " +
428→                        "side, e.g. parameter-list, type-argument-list, lambda-value-parameters, enum-entries, etc." +
429→                        "When set, IntelliJ IDEA uses this property to allow usage of a trailing comma by discretion " +
430→                        "of the developer. KtLint however uses this setting to enforce consistent usage of the " +
431→                        "trailing comma when set.",
432→                    PropertyValueParser.BOOLEAN_VALUE_PARSER,
433→                    BOOLEAN_VALUES_SET,
434→                ),
435→                defaultValue = true,
436→                defaultAndroidValue = false,
437→            )
438→
439→        @Deprecated(
440→            message = "Marked for removal in KtLint 0.49",
441→            replaceWith = ReplaceWith("TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_PROPERTY"),
442→        )
443→        @Suppress("ktlint:experimental:property-naming")
444→        public val allowTrailingCommaProperty: EditorConfigProperty<Boolean> =
445→            TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_PROPERTY
446→
447→        private val TYPES_ON_DECLARATION_SITE = TokenSet.create(

 15→/**
 16→ * AssertJ style assertion for verifying KtLint rules. This class is intended to be used as follows:
 17→ *
 18→ * ```
 19→ * // Create an assertThat assertion for a specific rule or set of rules. If possible define it at the class level
 20→ * val someRuleAssertThat = SomeRule().assertThat()
 21→ *
 22→ * @Test
 23→ * fun `Some test`() {
 24→ *     val code = .... // Original code to be formatted
 25→ *     val formattedCode = .... // The code as it should be formatted
 26→ *     someRuleAssertThat(code)
 27→ *         .hasLintViolation(1, 1, "some-error-description")
 28→ *         .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
 29→ * }
 30→ * ```
 31→ */

 44→     * that lint errors for those rules are suppressed.
 45→     */

 47→) {

 53→     * Set the [EditorConfigOverride] properties to be used by the rule. This function can be called multiple times.
 54→     * Properties which have been set before, are silently overwritten with the new vale.
 55→     */
 56→    public fun withEditorConfigOverride(

 64→    /**
 65→     * Set the [EditorConfigOverride] "max_line_length" property based on the EOL Marker which is places at the first
 66→     * line of the code sample. If the property has been set before via [withEditorConfigOverride] then that value is
 67→     * silently overwritten.
 68→     *
 69→     * Example of usage:
 70→     * ```
 71→     *  val code =
 72→     *      """
 73→     *      // $MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER                   $EOL_CHAR
 74→     *      val fooooooooooooooo = "fooooooooooooooooooooo"
 75→     *      """.trimIndent()

327→        assertAll(

560→    """
561→    The first line of the provide code sample should contain text '$MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER' which is provided by the
562→    constant '${::MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER.name}' and ends with the EOL_CHAR'$EOL_CHAR' provided by the constant
563→    '${::EOL_CHAR.name}' which indicates the last position that is allowed.
564→    """.trimIndent(),

 47→private fun Set<RuleProvider>.toRuleProviders(): Set<RuleProvider> {
 48→    val dumpAstRuleProvider = System
 49→        .getenv(KTLINT_UNIT_TEST_DUMP_AST)
 50→        .orEmpty()
 51→        .equals(KTLINT_UNIT_TEST_ON_PROPERTY, ignoreCase = true)
 52→        .ifTrue {
 53→            LOGGER.info { "Dump AST of code before processing as System environment variable $KTLINT_UNIT_TEST_DUMP_AST is set to 'on'" }
 54→            RuleProvider {
 55→                DumpASTRule(
 56→                    // Write to STDOUT. The focus in a failed unit test should first go to the error in the rule that is
 57→                    // to be tested and not to the AST,
 58→                    out = System.out,
 59→                )
 60→            }
 61→        }
 62→    return this.plus(setOfNotNull(dumpAstRuleProvider))

109→/**
110→ * Enables the rule sets for the given set of [ruleProviders] unless the rule execution of that rule set was already
111→ * provided.
112→ */
113→private fun EditorConfigOverride.extendWithRuleSetRuleExecutionsFor(ruleProviders: Set<RuleProvider>): EditorConfigOverride {
114→    val ruleSetRuleExecutions = ruleProviders
115→        .asSequence()
116→        .map { it.createNewRuleInstance().id }
117→        .map { ruleId -> createRuleSetExecutionEditorConfigProperty(ruleId) }
118→        .distinct()
119→        .filter { editorConfigProperty -> this.properties[editorConfigProperty] == null }
120→        .map { it to RuleExecution.enabled }
121→        .toList()
122→        .toTypedArray()
123→    return this.plus(*ruleSetRuleExecutions)
124→}

588→    private fun RuleExecutionContext.determineLineSeparator(fileContent: String): String {
589→        val eol =
590→            editorConfigProperties["end_of_line"]
591→                ?.sourceValue
592→        return when {
593→            eol == "native" -> System.lineSeparator()
594→            eol == "crlf" || eol != "lf" && fileContent.lastIndexOf('\r') != -1 -> "\r\n"
595→            else -> "\n"
596→        }
597→    }

604→     * `[*.{kt,kts}]`) new content.

631→    }

 15→import mu.KotlinLogging
 16→
 17→private val LOGGER = KotlinLogging.logger {}.initKtLintKLogger()
 18→
 19→/**
 20→ * The VisitorProvider is created for each file being scanned. As the [RuleRunnerSorter] logs the order in which the
 21→ * rules are executed, a singleton instance of the class is used to prevent that the logs are flooded with duplicate
 22→ * log lines.
 23→ */
 24→private val RULE_RUNNER_SORTER = RuleRunnerSorter()
 25→
 26→internal class VisitorProvider(
 27→    ruleRunners: Set<RuleRunner>,
 28→    /**
 29→     * Creates a new [RuleRunnerSorter]. Only to be used in unit tests where the same set of rules are used with distinct [Rule.VisitorModifier]s.
 30→     */
 31→    recreateRuleSorter: Boolean = false,
 32→) : UsesEditorConfigProperties {
 33→    override val editorConfigProperties: List<EditorConfigProperty<*>> = listOf(
 34→        KTLINT_DISABLED_RULES_PROPERTY,
 35→        DISABLED_RULES_PROPERTY,
 36→    ).plus(
 37→        ruleRunners.map {
 38→            createRuleExecutionEditorConfigProperty(toQualifiedRuleId(it.ruleSetId, it.ruleId))
 39→        },
 40→    )
 41→
 42→    /**
 43→     * The list of [ruleRunnersSorted] is sorted based on the [Rule.VisitorModifier] of the rules.
 44→     */
 45→    private val ruleRunnersSorted: List<RuleRunner> =
 46→        if (recreateRuleSorter) {
 47→            RuleRunnerSorter()
 48→        } else {
 49→            RULE_RUNNER_SORTER
 50→        }.getSortedRuleRunners(ruleRunners)
 51→
 52→    internal fun visitor(editorConfigProperties: EditorConfigProperties): ((rule: Rule, fqRuleId: String) -> Unit) -> Unit {
 53→        val enabledRuleRunners =
 54→            ruleRunnersSorted
 55→                .filter { ruleRunner -> isEnabled(editorConfigProperties, ruleRunner.qualifiedRuleId) }
 56→        if (enabledRuleRunners.isEmpty()) {
 57→            LOGGER.debug { "Skipping file as no enabled rules are found to be executed" }
 58→            return { _ -> }
 59→        }
 60→        val ruleRunnersToBeSkipped =
 61→            ruleRunnersSorted
 62→                .filter { ruleRunner ->
 63→                    val runAfterRules = ruleRunner.runAfterRules
 64→                    val runAfterRuleIds = runAfterRules.map { it.ruleId.toQualifiedRuleId() }
 65→                    runAfterRules
 66→                        .any {
 67→                            it.runOnlyWhenOtherRuleIsEnabled &&
 68→                                enabledRuleRunners.none { it.qualifiedRuleId in runAfterRuleIds }
 69→                        }
 70→                }
 71→        if (ruleRunnersToBeSkipped.isNotEmpty()) {
 72→            LOGGER.debug {
 73→                ruleRunnersToBeSkipped
 74→                    .forEach {
 75→                        println(
 76→                            "Skipping rule with id '${it.qualifiedRuleId}'. This rule has to run after rules with " +
 77→                                "ids '${it.runAfterRules.joinToString { it.ruleId.toQualifiedRuleId() }}' and will " +
 78→                                "not run in case that rule is disabled.",
 79→                        )
 80→                    }
 81→            }
 82→        }
 83→        val ruleRunnersToExecute = enabledRuleRunners - ruleRunnersToBeSkipped.toSet()
 84→        if (ruleRunnersToExecute.isEmpty()) {
 85→            LOGGER.debug { "Skipping file as no enabled rules are found to be executed" }
 86→            return { _ -> }
 87→        }
 88→        return { visit ->
 89→            ruleRunnersToExecute.forEach {
 90→                visit(it.getRule(), it.shortenedQualifiedRuleId)
 91→            }
 92→        }
 93→    }
 94→
 95→    private fun isEnabled(editorConfigProperties: EditorConfigProperties, qualifiedRuleId: String): Boolean =
 96→        // For backwards compatibility all different properties which affects enabling/disabling of properties have to
 97→        // be checked. Note that properties are checked in order of precedence. If a property of higher precedence has
 98→        // been defined, all properties with lower precedence are ignore entirely.
 99→        when {
100→            editorConfigProperties.containsKey(ktLintRuleExecutionPropertyName(qualifiedRuleId)) ||
101→                editorConfigProperties.containsKey(ktLintRuleSetExecutionPropertyName(qualifiedRuleId)) ->
102→                editorConfigProperties.isRuleEnabled(qualifiedRuleId)
103→
104→            editorConfigProperties.containsKey(KTLINT_DISABLED_RULES_PROPERTY.name) ->
105→                editorConfigProperties.isEnabled(
106→                    KTLINT_DISABLED_RULES_PROPERTY,
107→                    qualifiedRuleId,
108→                ) && ruleSetId(qualifiedRuleId) != "experimental"
109→
110→            editorConfigProperties.containsKey(DISABLED_RULES_PROPERTY.name) ->
111→                editorConfigProperties.isEnabled(
112→                    DISABLED_RULES_PROPERTY,
113→                    qualifiedRuleId,
114→                ) && ruleSetId(qualifiedRuleId) != "experimental"
115→
116→            else ->
117→                ruleSetId(qualifiedRuleId) != "experimental"
118→        }
119→
120→    private fun EditorConfigProperties.isRuleEnabled(qualifiedRuleId: String) =

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.core.internal
 2→
 3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.Rule
 4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.Rule.VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule
 5→import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.RuleProvider
 6→
 7→// TODO: Rename to RuleInstanceProvider. Also rename RuleRunnerSorter.
 8→internal class RuleRunner(private val provider: RuleProvider) {
 9→    private var rule = provider.createNewRuleInstance()
10→
11→    val qualifiedRuleId = rule.toQualifiedRuleId()
12→    val shortenedQualifiedRuleId = qualifiedRuleId.removePrefix("standard:")
13→
14→    val ruleId = rule.id
15→    val ruleSetId = qualifiedRuleId.substringBefore(':')
16→
17→    val runAsLateAsPossible = rule.visitorModifiers.contains(Rule.VisitorModifier.RunAsLateAsPossible)
18→    var runAfterRules = setRunAfterRules()
19→
20→    /**
21→     * Gets the [Rule]. If the [Rule] has already been used for traversal of the AST, a new instance of the [Rule] is
22→     * provided. This prevents leakage of the state of the Rule between executions.
23→     */
24→    fun getRule(): Rule {
25→        if (rule.isUsedForTraversalOfAST()) {
26→            rule = provider.createNewRuleInstance()
27→        }
28→        return rule
29→    }
30→
31→    private fun setRunAfterRules(): List<RunAfterRule> =
32→        rule
33→            .visitorModifiers
34→            .filterIsInstance<RunAfterRule>()
35→            .map {
36→                val runAfterRuleVisitorModifier = it as RunAfterRule
37→                val qualifiedAfterRuleId = runAfterRuleVisitorModifier.ruleId.toQualifiedRuleId()
38→                check(qualifiedRuleId != qualifiedAfterRuleId) {
39→                    // Do not print the fully qualified rule id in the error message as it might not appear in the code
40→                    // in case it is a rule from the 'standard' rule set.
41→                    "Rule with id '${rule.id}' has a visitor modifier of type '${RunAfterRule::class.simpleName}' " +
42→                        "but it is not referring to another rule but to the rule itself. A rule can not run after " +
43→                        "itself. This should be fixed by the maintainer of the rule."
44→                }
45→                runAfterRuleVisitorModifier.copy(
46→                    ruleId = qualifiedAfterRuleId,
47→                )
48→            }
49→
50→    fun removeRunAfterRules(runAfterRules: Set<RunAfterRule>) {
51→        require(!rule.isUsedForTraversalOfAST()) {
52→            "RunAfterRules can not be removed when RuleRunner has already been used for traversal of the AST"
53→        }
54→        this.runAfterRules = this.runAfterRules - runAfterRules
55→    }
56→}

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.core.internal
 2→
 3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.Rule
 4→
 5→internal fun Rule.toQualifiedRuleId() =
 6→    id.toQualifiedRuleId()
 7→
 8→internal fun String.toQualifiedRuleId(): String {
 9→    val ruleId = if (contains(":")) {
10→        this
11→    } else {
12→        "standard:$this"
13→    }
14→    // When IntelliJ IDEA is reformatting the ".editorconfig" file it sometimes add a space after the comma in a
15→    // comma-separate-list which should not be a part of the ruleId
16→    return ruleId.removeSpaces()
17→}
18→
19→internal fun toQualifiedRuleId(
20→    ruleSetId: String,
21→    ruleId: String,
22→): String {
23→    if (ruleSetId.contains(" ") || ruleId.contains(" ")) {
24→        return toQualifiedRuleId(ruleSetId.removeSpaces(), ruleId.removeSpaces())
25→    }
26→    return if (ruleSetId == "" || ruleId.startsWith("$ruleSetId:")) {
27→        ruleId.toQualifiedRuleId()
28→    } else {
29→        "$ruleSetId:$ruleId"
30→    }
31→}
32→
33→private fun String.removeSpaces() =
34→    this.replace(" ", "")
35→
36→internal fun ruleId(qualifiedRuleId: String) =
37→    qualifiedRuleId.substringAfter(":", qualifiedRuleId)
38→
39→internal fun ruleSetId(qualifiedRuleId: String) =
40→    qualifiedRuleId.substringBefore(":", "standard")
